demonstrated_acs.plain_outcome: The app loads in any standard browser over HTTP or HTTPS — no app install required.
A freelancer can complete the full Google sign-in flow and arrive at an authenticated session — no password entered.
demonstrated_acs.route: GET /auth/login -> HttpResponseRedirect(302 to OAuth) ; GET /auth/callback -> exchange_code -> handle_callback -> save_identity -> set_cookie(session) -> HttpResponseRedirect(/)

learnings: Google OAuth 2.0 external boundary remains mocked (identity_auth.oauth_provider.exchange_code via frozen conftest mock_oauth_provider) — real Google credentials and redirect-URI registration must be wired before production deployment.
Later-slice components C3 (Project Management), C4 (Time Logging), C5 (Invoice Export) are mocked via frozen conftest (CT9/CT10/CT11); these are the next capabilities to build and wire.

Data store uses in-memory dict at contract layer in tests; real PostgreSQL persistence (ADR-0003) is wired at integration — production database provisioning and connection-string configuration are a deployment prerequisite.
signoff: A — Yes, that's exactly what I wanted. The Google sign-in is clean and I like that there's no password to manage. Go ahead and build the next piece.
